(July 31, 1935 – April 7, 2015) was an American [actor](/wiki/Actor "Actor").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He appeared in more than 200 films and television shows, and was principally known for his film roles alongside [Clint Eastwood](/wiki/Clint_Eastwood "Clint Eastwood") and [Robert Redford](/wiki/Robert_Redford "Robert Redford").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He often portrayed [villains](/wiki/Villain "Villain") or [eccentric](/wiki/Eccentricity_(behavior) "Eccentricity (behavior)") characters.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor (1935–2015)

| Geoffrey Lewis | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | Geoffrey Bond Lewis  (1935-07-31)July 31, 1935  [Plainfield, New Jersey](/wiki/Plainfield,_New_Jersey "Plainfield, New Jersey"), U.S.[[1]](#cite_note-NYT-1) |
| Died | April 7, 2015(2015-04-07) (aged 79)  [Woodland Hills, California](/wiki/Woodland_Hills,_California "Woodland Hills, California"), U.S. |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Years active | 1953–2015 |
| Spouses | * Glenis Batley  ​  ​ (m. 1973; div. 1975)​ * Tracy Darroll  ​  ​ (m. 1977; div. 1984)​ * Paula Hochhalter  ​ (m. 1991)​ |
| Children | 9, including [Juliette](/wiki/Juliette_Lewis "Juliette Lewis") |

**Geoffrey Bond Lewis** (July 31, 1935 – April 7, 2015) was an American [actor](/wiki/Actor "Actor").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He appeared in more than 200 films and television shows, and was principally known for his film roles alongside [Clint Eastwood](/wiki/Clint_Eastwood "Clint Eastwood") and [Robert Redford](/wiki/Robert_Redford "Robert Redford").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He often portrayed [villains](/wiki/Villain "Villain") or [eccentric](/wiki/Eccentricity_(behavior) "Eccentricity (behavior)") characters.

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geoffrey_Lewis_(actor)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

Lewis was born July 31, 1935, in [Plainfield, New Jersey](/wiki/Plainfield,_New_Jersey "Plainfield, New Jersey"), but spent much of his youth in [Wrightwood, California](/wiki/Wrightwood,_California "Wrightwood, California"). He studied theater arts at [San Bernardino Valley College](/wiki/San_Bernardino_Valley_College "San Bernardino Valley College") for two years, then worked as a truck driver and at other odd jobs before launching his career as an actor. He took acting classes at the [Neighborhood Playhouse](/wiki/Neighborhood_Playhouse_School_of_the_Theatre "Neighborhood Playhouse School of the Theatre") in New York City and performed off-Broadway and at regional theaters in Massachusetts.[[4]](#cite_note-lat-4) He tried breaking into Hollywood in the 1960s.[[5]](#cite_note-imdb-5)

Lewis appeared in TV series including *[Bonanza](/wiki/Bonanza "Bonanza")*, *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")*, *[Mannix](/wiki/Mannix "Mannix")*, *[Mission: Impossible](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_(1966_TV_series) "Mission: Impossible (1966 TV series)")*, *[Cannon](/wiki/Cannon_(TV_series) "Cannon (TV series)"),*[[6]](#cite_note-6) *[Barnaby Jones](/wiki/Barnaby_Jones "Barnaby Jones")*, *[Mork & Mindy](/wiki/Mork_%26_Mindy "Mork & Mindy")*, *[The Golden Girls](/wiki/The_Golden_Girls "The Golden Girls")*, *[Lou Grant](/wiki/Lou_Grant_(TV_series) "Lou Grant (TV series)")*, *[Mama's Family](/wiki/Mama%27s_Family "Mama's Family")*, *[Magnum, P.I.](/wiki/Magnum,_P.I. "Magnum, P.I.")*, *[The A-Team](/wiki/The_A-Team "The A-Team")*, *[Titus](/wiki/Titus_(TV_series) "Titus (TV series)")*, *[Murder, She Wrote](/wiki/Murder,_She_Wrote "Murder, She Wrote")*, *[The X-Files](/wiki/The_X-Files "The X-Files")*, *[Little House on the Prairie](/wiki/Little_House_on_the_Prairie_(TV_series) "Little House on the Prairie (TV series)")*, *[Highway to Heaven](/wiki/Highway_to_Heaven "Highway to Heaven")*, *[Starsky & Hutch](/wiki/Starsky_%26_Hutch "Starsky & Hutch")*, *[Walker, Texas Ranger](/wiki/Walker,_Texas_Ranger "Walker, Texas Ranger")* and *[Law & Order: Criminal Intent](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Criminal_Intent "Law & Order: Criminal Intent")*. In 1979, he appeared in *[Salem's Lot](/wiki/Salem%27s_Lot_(1979_TV_miniseries) "Salem's Lot (1979 TV miniseries)")*. He also played opposite [Polly Holliday](/wiki/Polly_Holliday "Polly Holliday") in the *[Alice](/wiki/Alice_(American_TV_series) "Alice (American TV series)")* spin-off *[Flo](/wiki/Flo_(TV_series) "Flo (TV series)")* (1980–81)[[5]](#cite_note-imdb-5) for which he received a Golden Globe nomination.[[4]](#cite_note-lat-4) He co-starred with [Fred Dryer](/wiki/Fred_Dryer "Fred Dryer") in *[Land's End](/wiki/Land%27s_End_(TV_series) "Land's End (TV series)")* (1995–96).

His film credits include such movies as *[Down in the Valley](/wiki/Down_in_the_Valley_(film) "Down in the Valley (film)")*, *[The Butcher](/wiki/The_Butcher_(2009_film) "The Butcher (2009 film)")*, *[Maverick](/wiki/Maverick_(film) "Maverick (film)")*, and *[When Every Day Was the Fourth of July](/wiki/When_Every_Day_Was_the_Fourth_of_July "When Every Day Was the Fourth of July")*.

Lewis worked frequently with actor-director [Clint Eastwood](/wiki/Clint_Eastwood "Clint Eastwood") in several films, including *[Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil](/wiki/Midnight_in_the_Garden_of_Good_and_Evil_(film) "Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil (film)")*, *[Pink Cadillac](/wiki/Pink_Cadillac_(film) "Pink Cadillac (film)")*, *[Any Which Way You Can](/wiki/Any_Which_Way_You_Can "Any Which Way You Can")*, *[Bronco Billy](/wiki/Bronco_Billy "Bronco Billy")*, *[Every Which Way but Loose](/wiki/Every_Which_Way_but_Loose "Every Which Way but Loose")*, *[Thunderbolt and Lightfoot](/wiki/Thunderbolt_and_Lightfoot "Thunderbolt and Lightfoot")*, and *[High Plains Drifter](/wiki/High_Plains_Drifter "High Plains Drifter")*.[[5]](#cite_note-imdb-5)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In the 1980s, Lewis was also a member of musical storytelling group [Celestial Navigations](/wiki/Celestial_Navigations "Celestial Navigations") with musician and songwriter Geoff Levin.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Personal life and death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geoffrey_Lewis_(actor)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life and death")]

Lewis was married three times and had numerous children, with news outlets variously reporting that he had nine or ten surviving children.[[9]](#cite_note-9) One of his children is actress [Juliette Lewis](/wiki/Juliette_Lewis "Juliette Lewis")[[1]](#cite_note-NYT-1) with whom he acted in at least two films, *[Blueberry](/wiki/Blueberry_(film) "Blueberry (film)")* and *[The Way of the Gun](/wiki/The_Way_of_the_Gun "The Way of the Gun")*. His other children include his daughters Brandy, Hannah and Dierdre Lewis and Emily Colombier; his sons Peter, Lightfield, Miles and Matthew; and nine grandchildren.[[1]](#cite_note-NYT-1)

On April 7, 2015, he died of a [heart attack](/wiki/Heart_attack "Heart attack") at age 79. He had suffered the heart attack while working out at the [Motion Picture & Television Country House and Hospital](/wiki/Motion_Picture_%26_Television_Country_House_and_Hospital "Motion Picture & Television Country House and Hospital"), according to his family. Miles Lewis told *Variety* he had been suffering from [Parkinson's disease](/wiki/Parkinson%27s_disease "Parkinson's disease") and dementia.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
